exercises.title: PACKET TAG
exercises.brief: Stamp a label on a payload.
exercises.prompt:
Define **tag_packet(payload)** that returns the payload string with the prefix `[NET] ` in front of it.
~~~lua
tag_packet("ping")  -- "[NET] ping"
~~~
Build it with the **..** concatenation operator.
exercises.starter:
function tag_packet(payload)
  -- TODO: prefix with "[NET] "
  return ""
end
exercises.lore: Every packet wears the colors of its sender.

exercises.title: USER HANDLE
exercises.prompt:
Define **make_handle(user, node)** that joins the two arguments with an `@` between them.
~~~lua
make_handle("trinity", "zion")  -- "trinity@zion"
~~~
Use a **local** variable to build the result, then **return** it.
exercises.starter:
function make_handle(user, node)
  local handle = user
  return handle
end
exercises.solution:
function make_handle(user, node)
  local handle = user .. "@" .. node
  return handle
end
exercises.lore: A handle is the only true name in the wire.

exercises.title: STATUS LINE
exercises.prompt:
Define **status_line(core, level)** where `core` is a string and `level` is a number. Return the report:
~~~text
<core>: level <level>
~~~
For example `status_line("MAGI", 7)` returns `"MAGI: level 7"`. Concatenating a number with **..** converts it to text automatically.
exercises.starter:
function status_line(core, level)
  -- TODO
  return core
end
exercises.lore: The status line never lies. Only the operators do.

exercises.title: UPLINK LOG
exercises.prompt:
Store the call sign `"NEB-7"` in a **local** variable, then **print()** these three lines, in order, building each with **..**:
~~~text
Uplink: NEB-7
Channel open
NEB-7 ready
~~~
Use the local variable for the call sign in lines 1 and 3 (do not hard-code it twice).
exercises.starter:
local sign = "NEB-7"
-- TODO: print the three lines
exercises.solution:
local sign = "NEB-7"
print("Uplink: " .. sign)
print("Channel open")
print(sign .. " ready")
exercises.hint: print("Uplink: " .. sign) then print("Channel open") then print(sign .. " ready")
exercises.lore: Three lines and the hovercraft is yours.

exercises.title: SIGNAL RELAY
exercises.brief: Chain two helpers into one transmission.
exercises.prompt:
First define a helper **shout(text)** that returns `text` followed by `"!"`. Then define **relay(text)** that returns the result of `shout(text)` prefixed with `">> "`.
~~~lua
relay("go")  -- ">> go!"
~~~
Call `shout` from inside `relay` and combine the pieces with **..**.
exercises.starter:
function shout(text)
  -- TODO
  return text
end

function relay(text)
  -- TODO: prefix shout(text) with ">> "
  return text
end
exercises.lore: One signal, relayed, becomes a chorus.
